issues.description: Domain footnote 'arcsin/arccos: |x| < 1' is correct for the DERIVATIVE (undefined at endpoints) but a careful reader might confuse this with the function domain [-1, 1]. Mathematically the formula 1/sqrt(1 - x^2) is undefined at x = +/-1 since the denominator vanishes, so |x| < 1 is technically right for the derivative — but the slide should clarify that the FUNCTION arcsin x is defined on [-1, 1] while its DERIVATIVE is only defined on (-1, 1). Not an error, just imprecise.

The inverse-function derivative formula (f^-1)'(b) = 1 / f'(f^-1(b)) is stated without the crucial condition that f'(f^-1(b)) must be nonzero. Top AP students will be tested on cases where the formula fails (horizontal tangent on f -> vertical tangent on f^-1). Add a one-line caveat: 'Requires f'(f^-1(b)) != 0; otherwise the inverse has a vertical tangent there.' Also worth mentioning that f must be one-to-one (monotonic on the interval) for f^-1 to exist.

Solution dy/dx = -(2x + y)/(x + 2y) is shown without acknowledging that the slope is undefined where x + 2y = 0 (a vertical tangent on the curve x^2 + xy + y^2 = 7). A top student running the problem will notice the denominator and ask. Add a one-sentence note about the vertical-tangent locus.

Higher-order derivatives are only shown for an explicit polynomial. The AP exam frequently asks for d^2y/dx^2 on an IMPLICIT curve, which requires substituting the first-derivative expression back in. Either add a 30-second beat showing d^2y/dx^2 for x^2 + y^2 = 25, or explicitly flag this as a follow-up exercise.

FUN 3.5 'Selecting Procedures' is presented as a clean flowchart, but the meta-skill the AP actually tests is recognizing structure under time pressure when MULTIPLE rules nest (e.g., chain rule INSIDE a product rule INSIDE an implicit derivative). The slide says 'Most AP problems blend two or three of these' but doesn't show a worked blended example. Consider adding one mixed problem so students see the nesting in action.

decision_rationale: No critical issues from any reviewer and all AP-specific factual claims (Unit 3, FUN 3.1-3.6 topic codes, three named AP traps) are supported by the reference. However, Reviewer B raised four minor pedagogical gaps (most notably the missing f'(f^-1(b)) != 0 condition on the inverse-derivative formula) and Reviewer A flagged one minor imprecision on the inverse-trig domain footnote. With 2 reviewers reporting minor issues, the combined-decision rule yields REVISE — address the inverse-function caveat and the inverse-trig domain wording at minimum before shipping.
